title: Ongoing
subtitle: Multi-season prestige sci-fi series. Larger commitments that earn their length.

sections.name: I. High Priority
sections.desc: Strongest fits for your taste signal. Political/religious themes, character-first, smart structure.

items.title: Andor
items.whyPriority: Tony Gilroy (Michael Clayton) made grown-up Star Wars. Political, structurally rigorous, no Force/Jedi. Closest thing to The Conversation in this universe. Standalone-watchable — no Star Wars knowledge required.
items.pitch: Cassian Andor's path to becoming a rebel. Adult, political, structurally rigorous. The Star Wars project for people who don't usually watch Star Wars.

critics.quote: Widely treated as a high point of prestige TV in any genre.

critics.who: Multiple critics named S1 the best TV of 2022.
critics.quote: S2 finished in 2025 to similar acclaim.

items.title: Severance
items.whyPriority: Office workers undergo a procedure that severs work memories from personal memories. Closest modern descendant of The Prisoner. Bleak corporate dystopia with strange comic textures.
items.pitch: Ben Stiller directs most episodes. Multiple Emmy wins. Widely considered the best new prestige sci-fi series of the 2020s.

critics.quote: Strongly positive. Called it 'a precise, slow-building dread.'

items.title: Battlestar Galactica (2003 reboot)
items.whyPriority: Direct fit for your political/religious Dune signal. Post-9/11 allegory, AI ethics, religious conflict. Start with the 2003 miniseries — complete in itself. S1-3 widely considered as good as cerebral sci-fi TV gets.
items.pitch: Reimagining of the 1978 series as serious political drama. Set the template for sci-fi-as-political-allegory in the 2000s.

critics.quote: Named one of the 100 Best TV Shows of All Time.
Reviewed by prestige-TV critics with the seriousness usually reserved for HBO drama.

items.title: Foundation
items.whyPriority: Closest peer to Dune in scope and ambition. Asimov's psychohistory adapted with massive worldbuilding budget. Lee Pace as Brother Day is genuinely extraordinary. The Genetic Dynasty plotline alone is worth the watch.
items.pitch: Asimov's epic about a mathematician predicting the fall of empire. Loose adaptation — purists divided. The Cleon clones storyline is universally praised even by skeptics.

critics.quote: Mixed — some critics see it as a triumph of TV worldbuilding, others as overlong and uneven.
Widely considered one of the best in modern sci-fi TV.

items.title: Star Trek: Strange New Worlds
items.whyPriority: Best modern Trek by consensus. Episodic structure (rare in modern TV), optimistic, character-driven. Anson Mount as Pike. Strong fit for your character-first preference.
items.pitch: Captain Pike's Enterprise, pre-Kirk. Episodic stories let each premise breathe. Critics overwhelmingly positive.

critics.quote: Widely considered the best new Trek series since the franchise's modern relaunch. Episodic format praised as a return to Trek's strengths.

sections.name: II. Medium Priority
sections.desc: Strong probable fits with one or more variables.

items.title: The Expanse
items.whyPriority: Hard sci-fi space opera. Solar-system-scale political thriller. The most rigorous depiction of physics in TV space drama. Slow first three episodes; pays off enormously.
items.pitch: Belters, Martians, and Earthers. Adapted from the James S.A. Corey novels. Praised by working scientists for fidelity.

critics.quote: Widely considered the best hard-sci-fi TV series of its era.

items.title: Babylon 5
items.whyPriority: The original five-year-arc TV. Political/religious sci-fi years before Galactica. J. Michael Straczynski wrote 92 of 110 episodes alone. Aged production values; ageless storytelling.
items.pitch: Space station as diplomatic crossroads during a coming galactic war. Pioneered serialized TV with predetermined arcs.

critics.quote: Considered foundational to all serialized prestige TV that followed. Some critics rank it ahead of DS9 in ambition.

items.title: Star Trek: Deep Space Nine
items.whyPriority: The most morally complex Trek. Religious/political themes (Bajoran prophets, Cardassian occupation, Dominion War). The Trek series your Dune signal points toward most strongly.
items.pitch: Federation officers run a station near a wormhole. Serialized starting in S3. Avery Brooks's Sisko is the deepest Trek captain.

critics.quote: Widely considered the artistic peak of Trek by modern critics, especially for the Dominion War arc.

items.title: Fringe
items.whyPriority: Procedural-into-mythology done right. John Noble's Walter Bishop is a top-five sci-fi TV character. The pivot from monster-of-the-week to alternate-universe drama is well-executed.
items.pitch: FBI fringe-science division investigates X-Files-adjacent cases that resolve into a coherent multi-season mythology. JJ Abrams produced.

critics.quote: Built passionate following through DVD/streaming. The S2-S3 transition into the alternate universe is widely praised.

items.title: Lost
items.whyPriority: The character work is genuinely extraordinary even when the mythology frustrates. Final season divisive. Worth knowing as the show that defined 'puzzle-box prestige TV.' The Leftovers came directly out of its creators' regrets.
items.pitch: Plane crash survivors on a mysterious island. Character flashbacks slowly reveal the show is a sci-fi/fantasy thriller. Cultural watershed of mid-2000s TV.

critics.quote: Pilot widely considered one of the greatest in TV history. Final season polarizing — many critics defended it; many fans did not.

items.title: Mr. Robot
items.whyPriority: Tech-anxiety thriller, formally inventive (4th-wall breaks, framing experiments), Rami Malek's Emmy-winning performance. Sci-fi-adjacent — closer to The Conversation than to space opera. Smart structure.
items.pitch: A hacker with dissociative identity disorder is recruited by a shadowy collective. Sam Esmail wrote and directed every S2-4 episode.

critics.quote: S1 and S4 widely considered prestige-TV high points. Multiple Emmys including Outstanding Drama nominations.

sections.name: III. Stargate Threads
sections.desc: Your stated franchise interest. Stargate is genuinely large — listed in viewing order.

items.title: Stargate (1994 film)
items.pitch: The original Emmerich/Devlin film. James Spader as the linguist, Kurt Russell as the soldier. Foundation for the entire franchise.

critics.quote: Mixed at release; commercial hit. Spawned the most expansive sci-fi franchise of the 90s/00s outside Trek/Wars.

items.title: Stargate SG-1
items.whyPriority: The flagship show. Tonally lighter than BSG or DS9 — more procedural than political. Strong character ensemble. Unusually consistent quality across 214 episodes.
items.pitch: Air Force team explores planets through the gate network. Character-driven, episodic, occasionally arc-heavy.

critics.quote: Built a passionate fanbase. Saturn Awards repeatedly. Considered the most consistent of the major sci-fi franchises of its era.

items.title: Stargate Atlantis
items.pitch: SG-1 spinoff in the Pegasus galaxy. New cast, new threat (the Wraith). Stronger arcs than SG-1 in spots.

critics.quote: Considered the strongest of the SG-1 spinoffs by most fans, though without the cultural reach of the parent show.

items.title: Stargate Universe
items.pitch: Third spinoff. Tonally darker — closer to BSG than to SG-1. Cancelled before the story resolved. Worth knowing about; the cancellation is a real cost.

critics.quote: Polarizing among Stargate fans (too dark) but praised by prestige-TV critics. Cancellation widely lamented.

sections.name: IV. Star Trek — Modern Era
sections.desc: The post-2017 relaunch. Quality varies sharply by series.

items.title: Star Trek: Picard
items.pitch: S1 has a strong premise but is uneven. S2 is widely considered weak. S3 is a love letter to TNG and is widely praised. Skip S2.

critics.quote: Widely uneven across seasons. S3 considered redemptive by most TNG-era critics.

items.title: Star Trek: Discovery
items.pitch: Serialized, more dramatic than traditional Trek. Polarizing among Trek fans. Worth trying S1-2, then deciding.

critics.quote: Polarizing. Some critics champion the serialized approach; others find it too dark for Trek.

sections.name: V. Older Foundation
sections.desc: Pre-prestige-TV sci-fi that built the genre on television.

items.title: The X-Files
items.whyPriority: The mythology gets tangled by S6, but the standalone monster-of-the-week episodes are still some of the best TV ever made. Watch the highlight episodes if not the full thing.
items.pitch: FBI agents Mulder and Scully investigate paranormal cases. Cultural watershed of 1990s TV. Define your own watch order — there are episode guides for 'best of' viewings.

critics.quote: Routinely cited as one of the most influential TV series of the 20th century.

sections.name: VI. Weirder & Prestige-Adjacent
sections.desc: Shows that bend the genre or sit on its edges.

items.title: Westworld (S1 only)
items.whyPriority: S1 is genuinely excellent prestige sci-fi — puzzle-box structure, Anthony Hopkins, Thandiwe Newton. S2-4 widely considered to lose the thread. Watch S1 as a standalone.
items.pitch: Theme park populated by lifelike androids. S1 plays as a self-contained 10-hour film about consciousness and free will.

critics.quote: Multiple Emmy nominations. Widely considered one of the best HBO premieres in a decade.

items.title: The OA
items.pitch: A young woman returns after seven years missing, claiming she's been to other dimensions. Brit Marling co-wrote and stars. Cancelled before the planned five-season arc completed.

critics.quote: Polarizing. Critics like Emily Nussbaum strongly defended it; others found it pretentious. The cancellation provoked actual public protest.
